claim: The trigger across all four angles is the same: Synology's 2025-Plus drive lock-in pulled ServeTheHome's recommendation

Synology's 2025-Plus drive lock-in pulled ServeTheHome's recommendation , and even though DSM 7.3 reversed the 3.5″ HDD restriction in October 2025, M.2 SSDs remain HCL-gated and the trajectory is what convinced operators to leave

vethehome.com/synology-lost-the-plot-with-hard-drive-locking-move/), and even though DSM 7.3 reversed the 3.5″ HDD restriction in October 2025, M.2 SSDs remain HCL-gated and the trajectory is what convinced operators to leave
Hardware and OS pull in opposite directions on lock-in. The hardware survey rewards prebuilt boxes — UGREEN DXP4800 Plus at $620 with 10GbE and BYO-OS support

Hardware and OS pull in opposite directions on lock-in. The hardware survey rewards prebuilt boxes — UGREEN DXP4800 Plus at $620 with 10GbE and BYO-OS support , Aoostar WTR Max at $649-699 with 6 SATA + 5 NVMe + dual 10GbE SFP+
0 Plus at $620 with 10GbE and BYO-OS support , Aoostar WTR Max at $649-699 with 6 SATA + 5 NVMe + dual 10GbE SFP+ , Beelink ME mini at $209-329 idling at 7-10W
The OS survey scores TrueNAS Community Edition lowest on lock-in only at the app layer: the 24.10 k3s→Docker Compose rewrite with a hard 1 Jun 2025 auto-migration deadline
The OS survey scores TrueNAS Community Edition lowest on lock-in only at the app layer: the 24.10 k3s→Docker Compose rewrite with a hard 1 Jun 2025 auto-migration deadline
Unraid wins the volume layer (every array disk is a standalone XFS/btrfs/ZFS filesystem, mountable on any Linux box

Unraid wins the volume layer (every array disk is a standalone XFS/btrfs/ZFS filesystem, mountable on any Linux box ) but charges a paid licence; plain Debian + ZFS + Cockpit + zrepl wins everywhere except hand-holding
layer (every array disk is a standalone XFS/btrfs/ZFS filesystem, mountable on any Linux box ) but charges a paid licence; plain Debian + ZFS + Cockpit + zrepl wins everywhere except hand-holding
The migration playbook constrains hardware sizing. All three operational children agree the cutover is two-machine, side-by-side

book constrains hardware sizing. All three operational children agree the cutover is two-machine, side-by-side — you cannot reuse SHR pools in-place on TrueNAS or Unraid because mdraid+LVM+btrfs disks must be wiped and re-pooled
Hyper Backup repositories will not migrate — they are a proprietary deduplicated .HBK format readable only by DSM
Hyper Backup repositories will not migrate — they are a proprietary deduplicated .HBK format readable only by DSM , so move live data over rsync-in-tmux at ~140 MB/s
ECC is a red herring at home. OpenZFS docs recommend "8GB+", not the folkloric 1 GB-per-TB rule

ECC is a red herring at home. OpenZFS docs recommend "8GB+", not the folkloric 1 GB-per-TB rule , and Matt Ahrens's well-known position is that ZFS is no more ECC-dependent than any other checksumming filesystem
If your platform supports ECC (ASRock Rack B650D4U at ~$264 with onboard BMC
- Hardware: UGREEN DXP4800 Plus (turnkey, 4 SATA + 2 M.2, 10GbE, ~24W idle
- Hardware: UGREEN DXP4800 Plus (turnkey, 4 SATA + 2 M.2, 10GbE, ~24W idle , 2-year warranty

- OS + storage: TrueNAS Community Edition on ZFS, with raw zfs send -w replication chosen from day one (a single non-raw receive permanently blocks future raw incrementals
Pick Unraid 7.x instead if mixed-size disks and a 2000+ container app store outweigh ZFS's checksum guarantees

near-zero on DSM) the migrating operator is actually willing to absorb — and whether DSM 8 (still slipping past Q4 2025
